etymology.description: semantic 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 斿(斿) (you) -> concept of moving around
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement"), suggesting the action of moving, with the phonetic element 斿(斿) (you), which was associated with the idea of travel. This formation captures the sense of travelling or moving freely, as in wandering or floating, which connects to the meaning 'swim'.

examples.chinese: 孩子们在河里游泳(孩子們在河裡游泳)。
examples.english: The children are swimming in the river.

examples.chinese: 他经常去海边游泳(他經常去海邊游泳)。
examples.english: He often goes to the beach to swim.

examples.chinese: 小鱼在池塘里游来游去(小魚在池塘裡游來游去)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎoyú zài chítáng lǐ yóu lái yóu qù.
examples.english: The small fish swim back and forth in the pond.
